name: Mafra 1 - Green Route
description: This easy 8.5-mile touring cycling route in Mafra takes about 50 minutes, featuring the Mafra National Palace and José Franco Museum Village
region_intro: Touring cycling around Mafra offers diverse landscapes, from rolling hills and lush forests to winding coastal roads along the Atlantic Ocean. The region features varied terrain, including protected parkland like Tapada Nacional de Mafra and the more challenging Serra do Socorro area. Cyclists can explore routes with varying elevation gains, connecting historical sites with natural beauty. This blend of natural features and cultural attractions makes Mafra a compelling destination for outdoor activities. Best touring cycling routes around Mafra The most popular touring cycling route is Mafra 1 - Green Route, an easy 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail that takes about 50 minutes to complete. This route offers a gentle ride through natural scenery, including the Aldeia Típica José Franco. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Praia do Sul (Ericeira) – Mafra National Palace loop from Quinta das Pevides, a moderate 19.1 miles (30.7 km) path. This route connects the coastal town of Ericeira with the historic Mafra National Palace. Local touring cyclists also love the The Surfer – Fishermen's Beach ️ loop from Mafra, a 19.0 miles (30.6 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Touring cycling around Mafra is defined by coastal paths, rolling hills, and dense forests, with routes often passing by historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family rides to more challenging ascents. The routes in Mafra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 25 reviews. More than 170 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mafra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Mafra?
A: Mafra offers a diverse range of terrain for touring cyclists. You'll find everything from rolling hills and lush forests to scenic winding coastal roads along the Atlantic Ocean. The region includes protected parkland like &lt;strong&gt;Tapada Nacional de Mafra&lt;/strong&gt;, offering tranquil rides through nature, and the more challenging &lt;strong&gt;Serra do Socorro&lt;/strong&gt; area for those seeking steeper climbs and descents.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Mafra?
A: Yes, Mafra has routes suitable for all skill levels. For an easy and gentle ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2292772251/mafra-1-green-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mafra 1 - Green Route&lt;/a&gt;. This 8.5-mile (13.7 km) trail takes about 50 minutes to complete and passes through natural scenery, including the charming Aldeia Típica José Franco. Many routes feature minimal elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed family outing.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Mafra?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists looking for a challenge, the &lt;strong&gt;Serra do Socorro&lt;/strong&gt; area is known for its steep climbs and thrilling descents. A more demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32187164" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eco Caminho Bridge – Mafra National Palace loop from Mafra&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 38 miles (62 km) with significant elevation changes, requiring good fitness.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while cycling in Mafra?
A: Many routes in Mafra integrate historical sightseeing. You can cycle past the majestic &lt;strong&gt;Mafra National Palace and Convent&lt;/strong&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10346111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia do Sul (Ericeira) – Mafra National Palace loop from Quinta das Pevides&lt;/a&gt; connect coastal beauty with this architectural marvel. You might also encounter the traditional &lt;strong&gt;Aldeia Típica José Franco&lt;/strong&gt; or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2560455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forte do Milreu&lt;/a&gt; near Ericeira.

Q: Are there natural attractions or scenic viewpoints along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, Mafra is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the vast protected parkland of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6302068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tapada Nacional de Mafra&lt;/a&gt;, an 18th-century royal hunting ground with diverse wildlife. Coastal routes offer breathtaking views of the Atlantic, such as from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2668348" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz do Lizandro Beach Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the Miradouro da Praia do Sul in Ericeira. You might also discover the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/418105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anços Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2596769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerco Garden&lt;/a&gt; next to the Mafra National Palace.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by the coast or beaches in Mafra?
A: Absolutely. Mafra's proximity to the Atlantic Ocean means many touring cycling routes feature stunning coastal sections. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3958845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Surfer – Fishermen's Beach 🏖️ loop from Mafra&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, leading through picturesque coastal landscapes. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10346111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia do Sul (Ericeira) – Mafra National Palace loop from Quinta das Pevides&lt;/a&gt;, which connects the charming coastal town of Ericeira with historical sites.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Mafra?
A: The mild Mediterranean climate makes Mafra enjoyable for cycling for much of the year. However, spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November) are particularly recommended. During these seasons, temperatures are moderate, and rainfall is minimal, providing ideal conditions for touring cycling.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Mafra?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Mafra are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10346111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia do Sul (Ericeira) – Mafra National Palace loop from Quinta das Pevides&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3958845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Surfer – Fishermen's Beach 🏖️ loop from Mafra&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14659109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mafra National Palace – José Franco's Typical Village loop from EN 116 (X) R da Capeça&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Mafra?
A: The touring cycling routes in Mafra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 25 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse landscapes, which seamlessly blend coastal paths, rolling hills, and dense forests. The opportunity to combine natural beauty with historical landmarks like the Mafra National Palace is also a frequently highlighted aspect.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Mafra?
A: There are over 130 touring cycling routes available around Mafra, catering to a wide range of preferences and skill levels. This extensive network ensures that whether you're looking for an easy family ride or a challenging long-distance tour, you'll find suitable options.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through traditional villages or offer cultural insights?
A: Yes, some routes offer a glimpse into traditional Portuguese life. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2292772251/mafra-1-green-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mafra 1 - Green Route&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you past the &lt;strong&gt;Aldeia Típica José Franco&lt;/strong&gt;, a replica of an old Portuguese mini-village, providing a unique cultural experience during your ride.
tips: The 18th century Mafra National Palace is Portugal's largest monastery and was designed by a German architect.
Roadside attraction of a miniature Portuguese village with bakeries and restaurant. Great place for ceramic souvenirs.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle the Mafra 1 - Green Route?
faqs.answer: The Komoot estimated duration for this touring bicycle route is approximately 50 minutes. However, this can vary based on your cycling speed, fitness level, and how much time you spend enjoying the sights along the way.

faqs.question: Is the Mafra 1 - Green Route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is classified as 'easy' and is designed to be suitable for touring cyclists of varying fitness levels, including beginners. It features a generally gentle profile with an elevation gain of about 143 meters over its 13.7-kilometer distance.

faqs.question: Is the Mafra 1 - Green Route a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Mafra 1 - Green Route is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same general area, allowing for convenient access and return.

faqs.question: Where does the Mafra 1 - Green Route start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route typically begins and ends near the Mafra Kindergarten and Municipal Sports Park. While specific parking details aren't provided, this area in Mafra would be the most logical place to look for parking facilities.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Mafra 1 - Green Route?
faqs.answer: The route predominantly features paved surfaces, making it comfortable for touring bicycles. It includes sections that follow parts of the national road EN116, ensuring a smooth ride.

faqs.question: What cultural landmarks and sights can I see along the Mafra 1 - Green Route?
faqs.answer: This route offers a blend of cultural and rural scenery. Key highlights include the impressive Mafra National Palace at the start/end, and the José Franco's Typical Village in Sobreiro, which showcases traditional local life. The journey also passes through Achada.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal recommendations are not provided, generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for cycling in Portugal, avoiding the intense heat of summer or the cooler, wetter days of winter. Always check local weather forecasts before your trip.

faqs.question: Does the Mafra 1 - Green Route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is officially known as 'Mafra 1 - Percurso Verde (Fácil)' and covers 100% of that trail. It also briefly intersects with sections of 'Mafra 2 - Percurso Azul (Moderado)' and 'Volta ao Concelho 7 - Percurso Vermelho (Difícil)'.

faqs.question: Is the Mafra 1 - Green Route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is outdoors, specific regulations regarding dogs on this particular cycling path are not detailed. If cycling with a dog, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially on shared paths or near roads, and ensure they are comfortable with the distance and terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle the Mafra 1 - Green Route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and cycle the Mafra 1 - Green Route. It is a publicly accessible trail.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities near the Mafra 1 - Green Route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Mafra, a town with various amenities. Additionally, it passes through the José Franco Museum Village in Sobreiro and Achada, where you may find local establishments. It's advisable to carry water and snacks, but opportunities for refreshments should be available in the villages.
